title_en: 73. And in the same way a woman's touching a man without desire does not break
title_ar: الحديث الثالث والسبعون: لمس المرأة الرجل بغير شهوة
content_en: wudoo as is established from 'Aa'ishah (radhiAllaahu 'anhaa): I didn't find the Prophet (sallallaahu 'alaihi wa sallam) one night so I tried to find him with my hand - and my hand fell upon his feet which were raised up as he was making prostration. [Muslim (3/203) and an-Nasaa'ee (l/101)]. So we see from these two hadith that merely touching does not break the wudoo - and Allaah Knows best. The author of'at-Ta'leeqaat us-Salafiyyah' upon Sunan of an-Nasaa'ee (1/23) says concerning "he pushed me with his foot": It is well-known that that is a touching without desire. And so the author uses it as a proof that touching without desire does not break the wudoo . As for the touching with desire - its proof is that the wudoo is not broken until a proof is established that it does indeed break it - and this is enough of a proof to show that it doesn't break the wudoo for the one who holds that view - and above and beyond that is a further proof that it doesn't break the wudoo is the hadith of kissing - as normally kissing is not free from touching with desire. The author of 'Aun al Ma'bood' (1/69) says regarding her saying "he kissed me and did not make wudoo"' - It contains a proof that touching a woman does not break the wudoo as kissing is part of touching and the Prophet (sallallaahu 'alaihi wa sallam) did not make wudoo (before praying). And this (that touching a woman even with desire doesn't break the wudoo ) is the saying of 'Alee and Ibn 'Abbaas and 'Ataa ibn Taawoos, and Abu Hanifah and Sufyaan ath-Thawree. And this hadith is (in itself) da'if but is supported by other narrations. And it is mursal - however adDaaraqutnee joins it - and it is (therefore) an authentic (sahih) hadith inshaaAllaah. (See Nasb ur Raayah, l/70). The author of 'al-Mughnee' (1/190) says: The touching is not in itself something which breaks wudoo but breaks wudoo as it leads to prostratic fluid or semen being emitted - so the condition which leads to breaking of the wudoo is considered - and that is when there is desire. info@calgaryislam.com www.calgaryislam.com Summary: That when a man and woman are safe from anything being emitted which breaks wudoo - then their wudoo is not broken; and it is preferable to be on the safe side, as they may not be safe from such emissions in the condition of desire - and Allaah Knows best. CONCLUSION This is what Allaah ta'ala has made easy for us to gather together about the wudoo and its rulings, so we hope from Allaah the Most High, the All-Powerful, that we have been able to do justice to the subject - and we hope that any scholarly solicitous brother who comes across anything in our booklet which is incorrect to cover up and give sincere advice. And it is Allaah who grants success. REFERENCES:
title_id: Hadis 73: Penutup tentang Sentuhan Wanita dan Kesimpulan Risalah
content_id: Demikian pula, seorang wanita yang menyentuh laki-laki tanpa syahwat tidak membatalkan wudunya, sebagaimana ditetapkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu anha): Aku tidak mendapati Nabi ﷺ pada suatu malam, lalu aku mencarinya dengan tanganku, dan tanganku menyentuh kedua kakinya yang tegak sementara beliau sedang bersujud. [Muslim (3/203) dan An-Nasai (1/101)]. Dari dua hadis ini, kita melihat bahwa sekadar bersentuhan tidak membatalkan wudu. Adapun bersentuhan dengan syahwat, dalilnya adalah bahwa wudu tidak batal sampai ada bukti yang menunjukkan bahwa hal itu memang membatalkan wudu. Ini sudah cukup sebagai dalil bahwa hal itu tidak membatalkan wudu bagi mereka yang berpendapat demikian. Di samping itu, ada lagi dalil tambahan bahwa ciuman pun biasanya tidak lepas dari bersentuhan dengan syahwat, dan Nabi ﷺ tidak berwudu setelahnya. Penulis Aun al-Mabud (1/69) berkata tentang ucapan Aisyah "beliau menciumku dan tidak berwudu": Ini mengandung dalil bahwa menyentuh wanita tidak membatalkan wudu, karena ciuman adalah bagian dari bersentuhan, dan Nabi ﷺ tidak berwudu setelahnya. Ini adalah pendapat Ali, Ibnu Abbas, Ata ibn Tawus, Abu Hanifah, dan Sufyan Ats-Tsauri. Ringkasan: Apabila seorang laki-laki dan perempuan aman dari sesuatu yang dapat membatalkan wudu, maka wudu mereka tidak batal. Namun lebih baik berhati-hati, karena mereka mungkin tidak aman dari keluarnya sesuatu dalam kondisi syahwat. PENUTUP. Inilah yang Allah Taala mudahkan bagi kami untuk dikumpulkan tentang wudu dan hukum-hukumnya. Kami berharap kepada Allah Yang Maha Tinggi dan Maha Kuasa agar kami telah dapat memenuhi hak topik ini. Kami berharap agar setiap saudara yang berilmu yang menemukan sesuatu yang tidak tepat dalam risalah ini untuk menutupnya dengan nasihat yang tulus. Allah-lah yang memberi taufik.
content_ar: الوضوء as is established from 'عائشة (رضي الله عنها): I didn't find النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) one night لذا I tried to find him with my hand - and my hand fell upon his feet which were raised up as he was making prostration. [مسلم (3/203) and an-Nasaa'ee (l/101)]. لذا we see from these two الحديث that merely touching does not break the الوضوء - and Allaah Knows best. The author of'at-Ta'leeqaat us-Salafiyyah' upon Sunan of an-Nasaa'ee (1/23) says فيما يتعلق بـ "he pushed me with his foot": من المعروف that that is a touching without desire. And لذا the author uses it as a proof that touching without desire does not break the الوضوء . أما the touching with desire - its proof is that the الوضوء is not broken حتى a proof is established that it does indeed break it - and this is enough of a proof to show that it doesn't break the الوضوء for the one who holds that view - and above and beyond that is a further proof that it doesn't break the الوضوء is the الحديث of kissing - as normally kissing is not free from touching with desire. The author of 'Aun al Ma'bood' (1/69) says فيما يخص her saying "he kissed me and did not make الوضوء"' - It contains a proof that touching a woman does not break the الوضوء as kissing is part of touching and النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) did not make الوضوء (قبل praying). And this (that touching a woman even with desire doesn't break the الوضوء ) is the saying of 'Alee and ابن عباس and 'Ataa ibn Taawoos, and Abu Hanifah and Sufyaan ath-Thawree. And this الحديث is (in itself) ضعيف but is supported by other narrations. And it is mursal - غير أن adDaaraqutnee joins it - and it is (لذلك) an authentic (صحيح) الحديث inshaaAllaah. (See Nasb ur Raayah, l/70). The author of 'al-Mughnee' (1/190) says: The touching is not in itself something which breaks الوضوء but breaks الوضوء as it leads to prostratic fluid or semen being emitted - لذا the condition which leads to breaking of the الوضوء is considered - and that is عندما there is desire. info@calgaryislam.com www.calgaryislam.com Summary: That عندما a man and woman are safe from anything being emitted which breaks الوضوء - then their الوضوء is not broken; and it is preferable to be on the safe side, as they may not be safe from such emissions in the condition of desire - and Allaah Knows best. CONCLUSION This is what Allaah ta'ala has made easy for us to gather together about the الوضوء and its rulings, لذا we الأمل from Allaah the Most High, the All-Powerful, that we have been able to do العدل to the subject - and we الأمل that any العلمي solicitous الأخ who comes across anything in our booklet which is incorrect to cover up and give sincere advice. And it is Allaah who grants الفلاح. REFERENCES:
